He even went the extra mile by checking nearby stores for available products and sent me photos of the actual items, mentioning that there might be minor scratches, though none were visible in the photos.
I truly appreciated his genuine approach, product knowledge, and honesty.

My recent encounter with Croma has been nothing short of infuriating.
The glaring disparity between the showroom and online prices, amounting to a staggering 4500 rupees for a product worth around 15000 rupees, is utterly unacceptable.
Whats even more exasperating is the total lack of trustworthiness in Cromas online shopping system.
Their customer care is practically non-existent, and attempts to contact them were met with 15 minutes of futile waiting, only to be disconnected.
The call-back option proved equally unresponsive.
I initially held a deep regard for Tatas products and expected better from a brand of its stature.
However, my disillusionment with Croma has led me to one firm conclusion: Cromas system urgently needs a complete overhaul.
Such discrepancies and lack of customer support are not justifiable, and change is imperative.
Its high time Croma reevaluated its practices to regain trust and loyalty.
